Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Feb-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR #6576. Telephone: 415-551-0333.

Jason Rose, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Gary Kase, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Irene Kase, Deceased, and Holly Schuman, Melissa Kase-Elliott, Shannon Kase, Gary Kase, II, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Irene Kase, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default was entered against defendant on June 22, 2016.
Statement of Damages was filed and served on June 22, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 31, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Gary Kase, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Irene Kase, Deceased, and Holly Schuman, Melissa Kase-Elliott, Shannon Kase, Gary Kase, II, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Irene Kase, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Economic Damages: $184,601.50.
Non-Economic Damages - $13,000,000.00.
Total - $13,184,601.50.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
